hero.description: Unified oracle analytics platform delivering real-time price monitoring, cross-protocol benchmarking, and comprehensive performance metrics for Chainlink, Pyth, API3, and leading oracle networks. Empowering DeFi protocols, developers, and analysts with actionable insights.
hero.searchPlaceholder: Search trading pairs, oracles, or blockchains...
hero.noResults: No results found. Please try a different keyword.

features.sectionDescription: Explore core platform features for comprehensive oracle data analysis

bandCrossChainPriceConsistency.title: Cross-Chain Price Consistency Monitor
bandCrossChainPriceConsistency.subtitle: Band Protocol Cross-Chain Price Sync Status
bandCrossChainPriceConsistency.warningDesc: Some chains show deviation from the base price (Cosmos Hub). Please monitor price consistency.
bandCrossChainPriceConsistency.heatmapSubtitle: Visualize price deviation across chains and tokens
bandCrossChainPriceConsistency.bandMechanism1: Band Protocol implements cross-chain data transmission based on Cosmos IBC
bandCrossChainPriceConsistency.bandMechanism2: Cosmos Hub serves as the primary data source, transmitting price data to other chains via IBC
bandCrossChainPriceConsistency.bandMechanism3: Supports EVM chains (Ethereum, Polygon, etc.) to fetch data through Band Bridge
bandCrossChainPriceConsistency.bandMechanism4: Price deviation thresholds: <0.1% is normal, 0.1%-0.5% is warning, >0.5% requires attention

crossChainPriceConsistency.title: Cross-Chain Price Consistency Monitor
crossChainPriceConsistency.warningDesc: Some chains show significant deviation from the base price (Solana). Please monitor price consistency.
crossChainPriceConsistency.pythMechanism1: Solana serves as Pyth's main chain, prices are first published on Solana
crossChainPriceConsistency.pythMechanism2: Through Wormhole cross-chain messaging, prices are synced to Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, Optimism, Polygon and other chains
crossChainPriceConsistency.pythMechanism3: Prices across chains should remain highly consistent, deviation typically less than 0.1%
crossChainPriceConsistency.pythMechanism4: Large deviations may indicate network congestion or cross-chain message delays

confidenceAlert.noAlertsDesc: System is monitoring confidence interval changes

oraclePerformanceRanking.weightInfo: Weights: Response Time 30% | Accuracy 30% | Stability 20% | Data Sources 10% | Supported Chains 10%

responseTime.title: Response Time
responseTime.description: Average latency of data updates, shorter is better

accuracy.title: Accuracy
accuracy.description: Deviation of price data from benchmark price

dataSources.title: Data Sources
dataSources.description: Number and diversity of data sources

explanation.title: Volatility Explanation
explanation.stdDevDesc: Measures the dispersion of price data
explanation.cvDesc: Coefficient of Variation, the ratio of standard deviation to mean, used to compare volatility across different price levels
explanation.cvInterpretation: CV < 0.5: Extremely Low | 0.5-1: Low | 1-2: Medium | 2-3: High | > 3: Extremely High
explanation.rollingWindowDesc: Rolling Window: Calculates volatility using a sliding window to reflect changes over time
explanation.multiScaleDesc: Multi-time Scale: Calculates volatility for short-term (6h), mid-term (24h), and long-term (7d) separately
explanation.decompositionDesc: Volatility Decomposition: Breaks down total volatility into contributions from different time scales

explanation.title: Explanation
explanation.description: Price correlation measures the linear relationship between different oracle price data, ranging from -1 to 1.

cdfChart.title: Cumulative Distribution Function
cdfChart.subtitle: Shows cumulative probability distribution of latency values

latency.title: Price Update Latency Trend
latency.pythNotSupported: Pyth API does not support historical price queries, using mock data
latency.fetchError: Unable to fetch latency data, using mock data
latency.predictionDescription: Number of future prediction data points
latency.baselineDesc: Moving average of last 20 data points, representing current latency baseline
latency.stdDevDesc: Measures latency data volatility, 2σ covers ~95% of normal data
latency.aboutDesc: Price update latency reflects the time from Pyth Network oracle fetching price updates from data sources to on-chain availability. Normal latency should stay below 200ms. When latency spikes abnormally, it may indicate network congestion, slow data source response, or high system load. Monitor anomaly periods and assess impact on applications.

methodology.title: Data Methodology
methodology.subtitle: Learn about our price aggregation algorithms and risk assessment framework

aggregation.title: Price Aggregation
aggregation.description: Multi-source data fusion and price calculation
aggregation.intro: We employ multiple price aggregation algorithms that dynamically select the optimal approach based on data quality and market conditions, ensuring price accuracy and manipulation resistance.

simpleAverage.title: Simple Average
simpleAverage.description: Arithmetic mean of all valid data source prices, suitable for scenarios where data sources have similar quality.

weightedAverage.title: Weighted Average
weightedAverage.description: Assigns weights based on liquidity, trading volume, and historical accuracy of each data source for more accurate market price reflection.

median.title: Median
median.description: Takes the middle value of all prices, effectively resisting extreme values and anomalous data.

trimmedMean.title: Trimmed Mean
trimmedMean.description: Calculates average after removing highest and lowest percentages, balancing advantages of mean and median.

riskAssessment.title: Risk Assessment
riskAssessment.intro: Our risk assessment framework analyzes oracle data risk from multiple dimensions, helping users identify potential issues and make informed decisions.

centralization.title: Centralization Risk
centralization.description: Evaluates the decentralization level of data providers; higher concentration means greater risk.

freshness.title: Data Freshness Risk
freshness.description: Evaluates data update frequency and timeliness; stale data may lead to poor decisions.

deviation.title: Price Deviation Risk
deviation.description: Monitors price differences between data sources; abnormal deviations may indicate market manipulation or technical issues.

liquidity.title: Liquidity Risk
liquidity.description: Evaluates underlying market liquidity conditions; low liquidity may cause price distortion.

dataQuality.title: Data Quality
dataQuality.intro: We have established a complete data quality management system that continuously monitors and evaluates data quality from multiple dimensions.

completeness.title: Completeness
completeness.description: The completeness of data records, including field completeness and time series continuity.

accuracy.title: Accuracy
accuracy.description: The closeness of data to true values, assessed through multi-source comparison and historical validation.

consistency.title: Consistency
consistency.description: Consistency of data across different times and sources; abnormal fluctuations may indicate data issues.

timeliness.title: Timeliness
timeliness.description: The time delay from data generation to availability, particularly important for high-frequency trading scenarios.

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.intro: We use a comprehensive scoring model to calculate the confidence level of each data point, helping users assess data reliability.

dataSources.title: Data Sources & Verification
dataSources.intro: Our data comes from multiple tiers of sources, each with different verification requirements and credibility levels.
dataSources.primaryDesc: Directly from top-tier exchanges and official oracle networks, rigorously verified
dataSources.secondaryDesc: From reputable data aggregators and verified nodes, reliable quality
dataSources.tertiaryDesc: From community nodes and supplementary sources, for reference and cross-validation

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: The methodology provided on this page is for technical reference only. Actual data processing methods may be adjusted based on market conditions and technological developments. We are not responsible for any losses resulting from the use of data from this platform.

pageDescription: Comprehensive analysis of oracle market share, TVS trends and chain support
chartHint: Click chart elements to view details

navbar.marketOverviewDesc: Real-time market intelligence with comprehensive oracle TVL, market share, and protocol adoption metrics
navbar.priceQueryDesc: Instant price lookup across all major oracle networks with historical trend analysis
navbar.crossOracleDesc: Real-Time Multi-Oracle Price Comparison & Deviation Analytics
navbar.crossChainDesc: Cross-chain oracle performance benchmarking and reliability analytics
navbar.chainlinkDesc: Leading decentralized oracle infrastructure securing billions in TVL
navbar.bandProtocolDesc: Cross-chain data oracle powering Cosmos ecosystem applications
navbar.api3Desc: First-party oracle network with DAO-governed data feeds
navbar.umaDesc: Optimistic oracle enabling customizable data verification
navbar.redstoneDesc: Modular oracle stack with EVM+ compatibility
navbar.diaDesc: Transparent cross-chain oracle with crowd-sourced data validation
navbar.tellorDesc: Decentralized crypto-native oracle for transparency and decentralization
navbar.chronicleDesc: MakerDAO native oracle with Scuttlebutt security
navbar.winklinkDesc: TRON ecosystem oracle for gaming and DeFi

footer.description: Comprehensive analysis and comparison of mainstream oracle protocols, empowering Web3 developers and analysts to make informed decisions.

title: WINkLink
subtitle: TRON Ecosystem Oracle - Reliable off-chain data for gaming, DeFi, and entertainment

services.dataFeedsDesc: Decentralized price feeds for cryptocurrencies, commodities, forex, and more.
services.vrfDesc: Verifiable Random Function for provably fair and tamper-proof randomness.
services.automationDesc: Reliable smart contract automation for conditional execution.
services.ccipDesc: Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol for secure cross-chain messaging.
services.functionsDesc: Serverless Web3 platform for fetching data from any API.

ccip.feature1Desc: Send arbitrary messages between smart contracts on different chains with guaranteed delivery.
ccip.feature2Desc: Transfer tokens across chains using lock-and-mint or burn-and-mint mechanisms.
ccip.feature3Desc: Independent network monitoring cross-chain transactions for anomalies and security threats.
ccip.feature4Desc: Built-in rate limiting mechanisms to prevent network congestion and potential attacks.
ccip.feature5Desc: Support for major EVM chains including Ethereum, Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon, and more.

functions.feature1Desc: Write custom JavaScript code to fetch and process data from any API endpoint.
functions.feature2Desc: Functions are executed by multiple independent node operators for security and reliability.
functions.feature3Desc: Securely store API keys and credentials with threshold encryption for sensitive data.
functions.feature4Desc: Optimized execution pipeline ensures fast response times for time-sensitive applications.
functions.feature5Desc: Connect to any HTTP API, including Web2 services, without traditional oracle limitations.
functions.feature6Desc: Off-chain computation reduces on-chain gas costs while maintaining security guarantees.

description: UMA is an optimistic oracle that provides data verification for DeFi applications

autopay.fundQueryDesc: Sponsor deposits TRB to fund data queries
autopay.setParametersDesc: Define data type, frequency, and reward amount
autopay.autoRewardsDesc: Reporters automatically receive rewards for valid submissions

queryData.queryIdDescription: Query ID is a unique identifier for each data request in the Tellor network. It is generated by hashing the query data using keccak256.
